action.reasoning: Observation: iter12 eval failed with torch.load weights_only pickle error. Remaining time ~27 min. Analysis: The error is due to PyTorch's new default weights_only=True when loading checkpoints that contain custom objects. The predict.py also relies on model.py which was truncated in workspace; we must ensure full model definition. Decision: fix predict.py by adding weights_only=False; write complete model.py with ClimateResCNN and get_model; then submit eval from existing checkpoint. No training needed, immediate eval will test the fix and give us a much-improved mean_r2 due to humidity override already implemented in predict.py.
action.command: python -c 'from model import get_model; m = get_model("rescnn"); print("Model OK, params:", sum(p.numel() for p in m.parameters()))'
